title: Goodenia pusilla
intro: Goodenia pusilla, also known as Dwarf Goodenia, is a small, annual herb native to Australia, belonging to the Goodeniaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Goodenia pusilla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Goodenia pusilla is used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to help alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Goodenia pusilla is classified as a species within the family Goodeniaceae. This family, also known as the Goodenia family, is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. Goodenia pusilla is further classified as a member of the genus Goodenia, which consists of about 100 species of plants. The genus Goodenia is placed in the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that also includes sunflowers and daisies. Goodenia pusilla is also classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage.
